# OPC UA PubSub Subscriber for PHP

> Pure PHP OPC UA PubSub Subscriber (Part 14). Extension of `php-opcua/opcua-client`. Listens to UADP or JSON NetworkMessages over UDP unicast and multicast. Sign and SignAndEncrypt group-key security. All types use public readonly properties. `ext-sockets` required.

## What is this

A PHP library that consumes OPC UA PubSub streams. Publishers broadcast `DataSetMessage` payloads inside UADP or JSON `NetworkMessage` frames over UDP; this package decodes them, demuxes on `(publisherId, writerGroupId, dataSetWriterId)`, and hands them to the application as typed `DataSetMessage` DTOs with named fields. Supports PHP 8.2 through 8.5.

## Use cases

- Consume process variables broadcast by a PLC, SCADA, or historian via OPC UA PubSub
- Subscribe to UDP multicast streams on industrial networks
- Integrate PubSub data into PHP/Laravel/Symfony workers without a C extension
- Verify and decrypt signed and encrypted PubSub streams with pre-shared keys or a Security Key Service

## Key features

- UDP transport â€” unicast and IPv4 multicast (`MCAST_JOIN_GROUP` with legacy `IP_ADD_MEMBERSHIP` fallback), `SO_REUSEADDR`, non-blocking sockets, `socket_select` timeout, configurable receive buffer and TTL
- UADP codec â€” Part 14 Â§6.2 binary encoding. PublisherId Byte/UInt16/UInt32/UInt64/String, GroupHeader, PayloadHeader with one or more DataSetMessages, optional network-message timestamp. Variant, RawData, and DataValue field encodings. Chunking, promoted fields, and non-DataSet NetworkMessageTypes are rejected with a clear error
- JSON codec â€” reversible encoding from Part 14 Â§7.2 (encode + decode, no external metadata required)
- Subscriber runtime â€” event loop over N transports, demux by `(publisherId, writerGroupId, dataSetWriterId)`, blocking `run()` and non-blocking `poll(int $timeoutMs)`, signal-handler-friendly `stop()`
- PubSub security â€” Sign (HMAC-SHA256) and SignAndEncrypt (AES-256-CBC). Pre-shared keys via `StaticGroupKeyProvider` or SKS-backed rotation via `SksGroupKeyProvider` (calls `GetSecurityKeys` from Part 14 Â§8.4.2 through a classic `OpcUaClientInterface`). Supports `PubSub-Aes256-CTR` (32+32+4) and `PubSub-Aes128-CTR` (32+16+4) key layouts
- Metadata loaders â€” `DataSetMetaData::fromArray()`, `fromJsonFile()`, `fromXmlString()`, `fromXmlFile()`, `fromBinary()`, `fetchFromServer()`. XML loader accepts both compact `<DataSetMetaData>` and the OPC UA `<DataSetMetaDataType>` / `<FieldMetaData>` naming. Binary decoder walks the Part 14 Â§6.2.2 layout. `fetchFromServer()` reads a Variable through a connected classic client and honours already-decoded ExtensionObject codec results
- PSR-3 logging â€” any PSR-3 logger plugs in; `NullLogger` by default
- PSR-14 events â€” `NetworkMessageReceived`, `DataSetMessageReceived`, `DataSetFieldReceived`, `MessageDecodeError`, `SecurityValidationFailed`, `TransportOpened`, `TransportClosed`, `TransportError`. `NullPubSubEventDispatcher` by default (zero overhead)
- Pluggable transports â€” `PubSubTransportInterface` is the stable extension point. Third-party packages (MQTT, AMQP, ...) ship their own implementations without touching this package
- Module system â€” `PubSubModule` base class + `DataSetReaderModule` built-in. Custom modules register via `SubscriberBuilder::addModule()`
- MockClient-driven unit coverage â€” every codec round-trip, UDP loopback send/receive, kernel demux, builder wiring, security unwrap, metadata loaders, and SKS key fetch are covered without a real publisher
- Typed everywhere â€” all DTOs use public readonly properties

## Property access style

All Type classes and result DTOs use public readonly properties. Access is `$msg->fields`, `$field->name`, `$opts->interface` â€” not `$msg->getFields()` or `$opts['interface']`.

## Architecture

- SubscriberBuilder (src/SubscriberBuilder.php): entry point, fluent config (`setLogger`, `setEventDispatcher`, `onDataSetMessage`, `addModule`, `setCodec`, `useJson`). Terminals: `listenUdp(endpoint, transport, readers, security)` and `listenOn(transports, readers, security)`
- Subscriber (src/Subscriber.php): thin proxy implementing `OpcUaSubscriberInterface`. `run()`, `poll(int $timeoutMs): DataSetMessage[]`, `stop()`, `isRunning()`
- OpcUaSubscriberInterface (src/OpcUaSubscriberInterface.php): public API contract
- Kernel/ (src/Kernel/): event-loop infrastructure
  - PubSubKernel: polls transports, decodes, demuxes, dispatches events, invokes modules and user callbacks
  - PubSubKernelInterface: surface that modules depend on
  - NullPubSubEventDispatcher: zero-overhead PSR-14 fallback
- Module/ (src/Module/)
  - PubSubModule: abstract base
  - DataSetReaderModule: default built-in module carrying the reader configs
- Transport/ (src/Transport/)
  - PubSubTransportInterface: `open`, `close`, `poll`, `isOpen`, `transportUri`
  - ReceivedPayload: readonly DTO (`data`, `sourceUri`, `receivedAt`, `metadata`)
  - UdpOptions: readonly DTO (`interface`, `receiveBufferSize`, `ttl`, `reuseAddress`)
  - UdpTransport: ext-sockets implementation
- Encoding/ (src/Encoding/)
  - NetworkMessageCodec: codec interface
  - UadpNetworkMessageCodec / UadpDataSetMessageCodec: binary encoding
  - JsonNetworkMessageCodec / JsonDataSetMessageCodec: reversible JSON encoding
- Security/ (src/Security/)
  - PubSubSecurityMode: enum (None, Sign, SignAndEncrypt)
  - PubSubSecurityOptions: `unwrap(string): string`
  - GroupKeyProviderInterface: `signingKey`, `encryptingKey`, `keyNonce`, `refresh`
  - StaticGroupKeyProvider: pre-shared keys
  - SksGroupKeyProvider: calls `GetSecurityKeys` through a classic client
- Types/ (src/Types/): readonly DTOs and enums â€” NetworkMessage, DataSetMessage, DataSetField, FieldMetaData, DataSetMetaData, PublishedDataSet, DataSetReaderConfig, ReaderGroupConfig, PubSubConnectionConfig, FieldEncoding
- Event/ (src/Event/): 8 PSR-14 event classes
- Exception/ (src/Exception/): UnsupportedTransportException, PubSubDecodeException, InvalidDataSetReaderException, PubSubSecurityException

## Installation

```
composer require php-opcua/opcua-client-ext-transport-pubsub
```

Requires PHP >= 8.2, `ext-sockets`, and `php-opcua/opcua-client`.

## Quick example

```php
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\SubscriberBuilder;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Types\DataSetMetaData;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Types\DataSetReaderConfig;

$metadata = DataSetMetaData::fromJsonFile('/etc/opcua/line1.json');

$subscriber = SubscriberBuilder::create()
    ->onDataSetMessage(function ($msg) {
        foreach ($msg->fields as $field) {
            echo "{$field->name} = {$field->getScalar()}\n";
        }
    })
    ->listenUdp(
        endpoint: 'opc.udp://239.0.0.1:4840',
        readers: [new DataSetReaderConfig(
            publisherId: 100,
            writerGroupId: 1,
            dataSetWriterId: 1,
            dataSetMetaData: $metadata,
        )],
    );

$subscriber->run();
```

## Secured stream (pre-shared keys)

```php
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Security\PubSubSecurityMode;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Security\PubSubSecurityOptions;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Security\StaticGroupKeyProvider;

$subscriber = SubscriberBuilder::create()
    ->listenUdp(
        endpoint: 'opc.udp://239.0.0.1:4840',
        security: new PubSubSecurityOptions(
            mode: PubSubSecurityMode::SignAndEncrypt,
            keyProvider: new StaticGroupKeyProvider(
                signingKey: hex2bin($_ENV['PUBSUB_SIGN']),
                encryptingKey: hex2bin($_ENV['PUBSUB_ENC']),
                keyNonce: hex2bin($_ENV['PUBSUB_NONCE']),
            ),
        ),
        readers: [$reader],
    );
```

## Non-blocking polling

```php
while (! $shouldStop) {
    foreach ($subscriber->poll(timeoutMs: 250) as $msg) {
        handle($msg);
    }
    doOtherWork();
}
```

## Custom transport

Implement `PubSubTransportInterface` and pass the instance via `SubscriberBuilder::listenOn(transports: [$yours], readers: [...])`. The subscriber is agnostic to the underlying transport â€” this is how `php-opcua/opcua-client-ext-mqtt` plugs in.

## Requirements

- PHP >= 8.2
- ext-sockets
- ext-openssl (transitive, required by the core)
- php-opcua/opcua-client ^4.4
- psr/log ^3.0 and psr/event-dispatcher ^1.0 (interface-only)

## Repository

https://github.com/php-opcua/opcua-client-ext-transport-pubsub
